title: oscore: MemorySecurityContext may not implement all required aiocoap interfaces
description:
File: python/src/lichen/crypto/oscore.py
Lines: 45

MemorySecurityContext inherits from:
```python
class MemorySecurityContext(CanProtect, CanUnprotect, SecurityContextUtils):
```

It implements:
- new_sequence_number() - line 136-150
- post_seqnoincrease() - line 152-154
- Initializes via derive_keys() from SecurityContextUtils

However, depending on aiocoap version, the CanProtect and CanUnprotect protocols may require additional methods or properties that are not explicitly implemented:

1. No protect() or unprotect() methods - relies on SecurityContextUtils
2. No context_id or kid_context properties
3. No recipient_key, sender_key, common_iv directly exposed (set by derive_keys)

If aiocoap updates its OSCORE interfaces, this class may break silently. The class should either:
1. Document which aiocoap version it's compatible with
2. Add explicit abstract method implementations to catch interface changes

Impact: Potential silent breakage on aiocoap updates. Hard to diagnose integration failures.
close_reason: Added explicit interface elements (class attrs for is_signing etc., properties for context_id/kid_context, documented aiocoap 0.4.17 compatibility); ruff/mypy pass; tests for direct construction pass (EDHOC tests blocked by unrelated edhoc.py issue)
